highlights.text: My general lean is we can backtest 6505, then 6518-20.
The core of these is a two part cycle I call “Elevator Down-into-Short Squeeze”.
When it sells, it is always vertical, and “elevator down”.
My core setup: The Failed Breakdown.
This was the story of last week: “Elevator Down” last Tuesday when ES lost a week long support shelf at 6461 and we sold down to 6371 low of week.
I tweeted at 11:55AM last Tuesday that the 6384 Failed Breakdown would squeeze us (6384 was a major low Tuesday morning), and up we went.
On Friday, NFP hit and we restarted the cycle, going “elevator down” selling from 6540 to 6452 low.

I wrote on on Thursday at 4pm that “Bulls want to see us defend the supports mentioned above on any dips (no lower than 6461 or trap below) .” On Friday at 11am we trapped under 6461, Failed Breakdown, rallied to 6490 to close last week The technical task for bulls today was to keep that dip buy momentum going and back-test the regions we broke down from Friday and ideally, recover them to invalidate the Friday breakdown.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the Failed Breakdown that gave us last weeks 100+ point squeeze (its important to know), then I’ll discuss the actionable trade plan for Tuesday.

post_title: SPX Is Again Coiled Tight For A Move, With Catalysts Coming. What Way? Sept 9th Plan
